name: Sarah Bury
bio: Sarah Bury (age 42) is a Rogers Park-based attorney, environmental advocate, and organizer challenging three incumbents for one of the three open MWRD 6-year term seats. She earned a joint JD/MA in environmental law and policy from American University (Washington, DC), with internships at the City of Chicago legal department, U.S. EPA, Center for International Environmental Law, and DOJ. She grew up in a home that flooded and has experienced basement flooding in multiple Cook County neighborhoods. She previously ran for MWRD in 2019 and applied for a board appointment in 2024.
prior_office: Student Representative, Whitney Young High School Local School Council (early 2000s). No prior elected office held. Candidate for MWRD Commissioner (2019, did not win); applicant for MWRD board appointment (2024).

jacket.note: Grassroots campaign; likely small-dollar fundraising compared to incumbents

key_votes.bill: First-time candidate — environmental lawyer, no prior elected office

policy_platform.position: Government accountability and transparency at MWRD
Facilitating public participation in MWRD decisions
Clean water access and flood prevention
Environmental justice with focus on working-class and diverse communities
Back to Basics: ensuring MWRD fulfills core mission responsibly

career_history.role: Senior Project Manager / Attorney (current) — works in discovery on corporate litigation matters
Dale Bryson Water Quality Fellow, Alliance for the Great Lakes — drafted letters on water pollution, compared phosphorus laws across Midwest states
Water Issue Specialist, League of Women Voters of Illinois — tracked water-related legislation
Vice President (now Treasurer), League of Women Voters Lake Michigan Region
Volunteer, Sierra Club Chicago Water Team — monitored stormwater permit compliance, tested Chicago River for phosphorus and dissolved oxygen
Field Organizer, Ald. Maria Hadden campaign (49th Ward); Bring Chicago Home ballot campaign (2023–2024)
Volunteer Management, Operation Swing State (Kamala Harris campaign 2024)
Collected signatures for current MWRD Commissioner Cam Davis alongside former Commissioner Debra Shore
Attended UNFCCC Conference of Parties (UN climate negotiations)
JD/MA, American University, internships at City of Chicago legal dept, US EPA, CIEL, DOJ
